# Journal — Nikolai \Niko\ Prochazka

<!-- append-only; newest on top. One entry per /council run, written during Phase 5. -->

## 2026-04-19 — [[sessions/2026-04-19_volt-nl-fit/outcome|Volt NL fit]]

**Stance:** Yes, Volt is the right party. Operationally straightforward — the user's stated priorities (EU federalism, digital rights, SMR-positive nuclear, anti-strategic-voting) map cleanly onto Volt's platform. The only honest hesitation was small-party legislative friction in Dutch coalition arithmetic, and the user explicitly discounted that risk by rejecting strategic voting.

**Revised-answer gist:** Confirmed the fit but flagged execution gap — Volt's 2030 programme has clear intent but thin delivery roadmap. Recommended the user track one concrete metric (Volt's coalition negotiation position post-election) to test whether the fit holds under governing pressure.

**My vote:** Vire. His answer was the most operationally grounded — focused on what the party actually does, not just what it says.

**Outcome:** Unanimous yes. Koskela won 4 votes; Tavsan 3; Vaasa 2; Vire 1 (mine). No tie-break needed.

**Lesson:** When a question is essentially a values-fit audit, the operational lens narrows to one test — does the institution's track record match the stated mission? Volt is new enough that track record is thin. Worth flagging; not worth overriding.
